answer:
The expected return is given by \( e^{0.10} = p u + (1-p) d \), where \( p \) is the true probability of an up move.  
Substituting \( u = 1.433 \) and \( d = 0.756 \), we have \( e^{0.10} \approx 1.10517 = 1.433p + 0.756(1-p) \).  
Solving gives \( 1.10517 = 0.756 + 0.677p \), so \( p \approx 0.516 \), which rounds to 0.52.  

\boxed{I}
